description: Phase 0 cross-project orchestration: portfolio management, cross-project milestones, dependency analysis, and critical-path computation as a CLI-side meta-construct. Uses existing Kanboard API (task links, metadata) as persistence layer — no server-side plugin required for core functionality. Adds `src/kanboard/orchestration/` subpackage, 4 new models, 2 CLI command groups, and comprehensive test coverage.

userStories.title: Orchestration subpackage scaffolding
userStories.description: Create the src/kanboard/orchestration/ subpackage with __init__.py, portfolio.py, dependencies.py, and store.py stub modules. The orchestration classes are instantiated separately (not wired into KanboardClient as attributes) — they accept a KanboardClient as a constructor argument. Update tests/test_smoke.py to include importability checks for the new package.
acceptanceCriteria: src/kanboard/orchestration/__init__.py exports PortfolioManager, DependencyAnalyzer, LocalPortfolioStore
src/kanboard/orchestration/portfolio.py, dependencies.py, and store.py exist with stub classes
from kanboard.orchestration import PortfolioManager, DependencyAnalyzer, LocalPortfolioStore succeeds
Orchestration classes are NOT wired into KanboardClient as attributes (opt-in, not auto-attached)
tests/test_smoke.py updated with importability assertions for orchestration classes
tests/unit/orchestration/__init__.py created for the new test directory
ruff check . && ruff format --check . passes
pytest passes
userStories.notes: Milestones 1-4 are complete on main. This begins Phase 0. Do NOT add orchestration to KanboardClient — it is opt-in, instantiated separately by callers.

userStories.title: Orchestration data models
userStories.description: Add Portfolio, Milestone, MilestoneProgress, and DependencyEdge dataclasses to src/kanboard/models.py. These models do NOT have from_api() classmethods — they are composed client-side from multiple API responses, not deserialized from a single API response. Portfolio and Milestone are mutable (no frozen=True). Update kanboard/__init__.py to export the 4 new models.
acceptanceCriteria: Portfolio dataclass added: name, description, project_ids: list[int], milestones: list[Milestone], created_at: datetime | None, updated_at: datetime | None
Milestone dataclass added: name, portfolio_name, target_date: datetime | None, task_ids: list[int], critical_task_ids: list[int]
MilestoneProgress dataclass added: milestone_name, portfolio_name, target_date, total, completed, percent: float, is_at_risk: bool, is_overdue: bool, blocked_task_ids: list[int]

DependencyEdge dataclass added: task_id, task_title, task_project_id, task_project_name, opposite_task_id, opposite_task_title, opposite_task_project_id, opposite_task_project_name, link_label, is_cross_project: bool, is_resolved: bool
No from_api() classmethods on any of the 4 models (composed client-side, not deserialized)
All 4 models exported from kanboard/__init__.py __all__ (sorted)
Existing model tests still pass
ruff check . && ruff format --check . passes
pytest passes
userStories.notes: Portfolio/Milestone are mutable (edited locally before syncing). MilestoneProgress and DependencyEdge are computed/read-only but kept mutable for simplicity.

userStories.title: Local portfolio store — JSON persistence
userStories.description: Implement src/kanboard/orchestration/store.py with LocalPortfolioStore — full CRUD for portfolios, milestones, and milestone task membership backed by ~/.config/kanboard/portfolios.json. Uses stdlib json only. Atomic writes (write temp, rename). Uses CONFIG_DIR constant from kanboard.config. Raises KanboardConfigError on malformed JSON or schema version mismatch.
acceptanceCriteria: LocalPortfolioStore.__init__(path: Path | None = None) defaults to CONFIG_DIR / 'portfolios.json'
load() -> list[Portfolio] returns [] when file does not exist
save(portfolios) writes atomically (temp file then rename) to prevent corruption
create_portfolio(name, description, project_ids) raises ValueError on duplicate name
get_portfolio(name) raises KanboardConfigError when not found
update_portfolio(name, **kwargs) updates fields in-place and saves
remove_portfolio(name) returns True on success, False if not found
add_project / remove_project update project_ids list and save
add_milestone / update_milestone / remove_milestone manage milestones within a portfolio
add_task_to_milestone(portfolio_name, milestone_name, task_id, critical=False) adds to task_ids and optionally critical_task_ids
remove_task_from_milestone removes from both task_ids and critical_task_ids
datetime fields serialized as ISO-8601 strings and deserialized with datetime.fromisoformat()
File and parent directories created automatically on first write
Unit tests in tests/unit/orchestration/test_store.py using tmp_path fixture (no real filesystem)
>=95% coverage on store.py
ruff check . && ruff format --check . passes
pytest passes
userStories.notes: Portfolio names are case-sensitive and unique. Milestone names are unique within a portfolio. File format version field is 1. Use json stdlib only — no external deps.

userStories.title: Portfolio manager — multi-project aggregation
userStories.description: Implement src/kanboard/orchestration/portfolio.py with PortfolioManager. Accepts KanboardClient and LocalPortfolioStore as constructor arguments. Aggregates task data across multiple projects, computes milestone progress, and syncs portfolio/milestone membership to Kanboard metadata using the kanboard_cli: key prefix convention.
acceptanceCriteria: PortfolioManager.__init__(client: KanboardClient, store: LocalPortfolioStore)
get_portfolio_projects(portfolio_name) -> list[Project]: fetches each project_id via client.projects.get_project_by_id()
get_portfolio_tasks(portfolio_name, status=1, assignee_id=None, project_id=None) -> list[Task]: iterates project IDs, calls get_all_tasks per project, applies optional filters
get_milestone_progress(portfolio_name, milestone_name) -> MilestoneProgress: fetches each task, counts closed vs total, computes percent
MilestoneProgress.is_overdue = target_date < now and percent < 100
MilestoneProgress.is_at_risk = target_date within 7 days and percent < 80
MilestoneProgress.blocked_task_ids populated from tasks with open blockers (uses client.task_links)
get_all_milestone_progress(portfolio_name) -> list[MilestoneProgress]
sync_metadata(portfolio_name) writes kanboard_cli:portfolio JSON to each project's metadata and kanboard_cli:milestones JSON to each task's metadata; returns {'projects_synced': N, 'tasks_synced': N}
Missing projects/tasks (deleted from Kanboard but still in portfolio) handled gracefully — logged warning, skipped
Unit tests in tests/unit/orchestration/test_portfolio.py mocking KanboardClient with pytest-httpx
>=90% coverage on portfolio.py
ruff check . && ruff format --check . passes
pytest passes
userStories.notes: N+1 API calls by design — Phase 1 plugin will solve server-side. Metadata keys: kanboard_cli:portfolio (project scope), kanboard_cli:milestones (task scope), kanboard_cli:milestone_critical (task scope).

userStories.title: Dependency analyzer — graph traversal and critical path
userStories.description: Implement src/kanboard/orchestration/dependencies.py with DependencyAnalyzer. Builds directed dependency graphs from Kanboard internal task links (blocks/is blocked by). Uses topological sort (Kahn's algorithm) for critical path computation. Caches fetched tasks to avoid redundant API calls. Deduplicates bidirectional edges.
acceptanceCriteria: DependencyAnalyzer.__init__(client: KanboardClient)
get_dependency_edges(tasks, cross_project_only=False) -> list[DependencyEdge]: fetches task links for each task, filters to blocks/is-blocked-by relationships, enriches with project names
Edges deduplicated — task A blocks task B produces one edge, not two (from querying both A and B)
get_blocked_tasks(tasks) -> list[tuple[Task, list[DependencyEdge]]]: tasks with unresolved (open) blockers
get_blocking_tasks(tasks) -> list[tuple[Task, list[DependencyEdge]]]: open tasks blocking other tasks
get_critical_path(tasks) -> list[Task]: longest dependency chain via topological sort + longest-path DAG
Critical path considers only unresolved (open) tasks
Cycle detection: log warning and return partial result (defensive, should not occur with blocks/blocked-by)
get_dependency_graph(tasks, cross_project_only=False) -> dict with 'nodes' and 'edges' keys
Link types looked up by label ('blocks', 'is blocked by') not hardcoded IDs
Task enrichment cached in dict[int, Task] to avoid redundant get_task() calls
Unit tests covering: linear chain, diamond dependency, no dependencies, cross-project filter, critical path (single chain, parallel chains, empty), cycle detection, resolved vs unresolved edges
>=90% coverage on dependencies.py
ruff check . && ruff format --check . passes
pytest passes
userStories.notes: getAllTaskLinks returns links from the queried task's perspective — must deduplicate when processing both ends of a relationship. Link IDs 2/3 are conventional but look up by label for robustness.

userStories.title: ASCII dependency graph and progress renderers
userStories.description: Implement src/kanboard_cli/renderers.py with four render functions: render_dependency_graph (text-based dependency visualization grouped by project with arrow annotations), render_critical_path (numbered sequential list), render_milestone_progress (Unicode progress bar with at-risk/overdue indicators), and render_portfolio_summary (portfolio dashboard). Uses rich.console.Console for color support.
acceptanceCriteria: render_dependency_graph(edges, nodes, cross_project_only=False, use_color=True) -> str: groups nodes by project, shows blocking arrows, uses ANSI colors (green=resolved, red=blocked, yellow=open)
render_critical_path(tasks, edges) -> str: numbered sequential list identifying the bottleneck task
render_milestone_progress(progress, use_color=True) -> str: 20-char Unicode progress bar (█/░), at-risk ⚠ prefix, overdue 🔴 suffix, target date shown
render_portfolio_summary(portfolio, milestones, task_count, blocked_count) -> str: dashboard summary
Color output can be disabled via use_color=False (for piping, CI, quiet mode)
Progress bar width: 20 chars, fill = round(percent / 5) blocks
Unit tests verify output contains expected task references, project names, and formatting markers (not exact character-by-character)
ruff check . && ruff format --check . passes
pytest passes
userStories.notes: Dependency graph renderer is a simple text list — not a full graph layout engine. Format: project header, indented tasks with status icons and arrow annotations showing blocks/blocked-by relationships.

userStories.title: Portfolio CLI command group — CRUD and aggregation
userStories.description: Create src/kanboard_cli/commands/portfolio.py with 8 subcommands: list, show, create, remove, add-project, remove-project, tasks, sync. Register in main.py. Portfolio show combines local store data with live API data and displays milestone progress bars. Portfolio tasks supports all 4 output formats.
acceptanceCriteria: kanboard portfolio list: table of name, description, project count, milestone count from local store
kanboard portfolio show <name>: portfolio summary + milestone progress bars + at-risk items (uses renderers from US-006)
kanboard portfolio create <name> [--description TEXT]: creates in local store, success message
kanboard portfolio remove <name> --yes: removes from local store + best-effort metadata cleanup, requires --yes
kanboard portfolio add-project <name> <project_id>: validates project exists via API then adds to store
kanboard portfolio remove-project <name> <project_id> --yes: removes from store, requires --yes

kanboard portfolio tasks <name> [--status active|closed] [--project PROJECT_ID] [--assignee USER_ID]: all 4 output formats; table includes id, title, project_name, column_title, owner_username, date_due, priority
kanboard portfolio sync <name>: calls sync_metadata(), prints 'Synced N projects, N tasks'
portfolio not found raises clear ClickException with portfolio name in message
Registered in src/kanboard_cli/main.py
CLI tests in tests/cli/test_portfolio.py covering all 8 subcommands with mocked client and store
ruff check . && ruff format --check . passes
pytest passes
userStories.notes: portfolio show: if API is unreachable, show cached store data with a warning. portfolio tasks table requires fetching project names — cache project lookups. portfolio create uses positional argument for name, not --name option.

userStories.title: Milestone CLI command group
userStories.description: Create src/kanboard_cli/commands/milestone.py with 7 subcommands: list, show, create, remove, add-task, remove-task, progress. Register in main.py. Milestone add-task validates the task belongs to a portfolio project before adding.
acceptanceCriteria: kanboard milestone list <portfolio_name>: table of name, target_date, task_count, critical_count from local store
kanboard milestone show <portfolio_name> <milestone_name>: progress bar + task list with status indicators and blocker info
kanboard milestone create <portfolio_name> <milestone_name> [--target-date YYYY-MM-DD] [--description TEXT]
kanboard milestone remove <portfolio_name> <milestone_name> --yes: removes milestone + cleans task metadata, requires --yes
kanboard milestone add-task <portfolio_name> <milestone_name> <task_id> [--critical]: validates task exists and task.project_id is in portfolio project list before adding
kanboard milestone remove-task <portfolio_name> <milestone_name> <task_id> --yes: requires --yes
kanboard milestone progress <portfolio_name> [milestone_name]: with milestone_name shows single detail; without shows all milestones in portfolio via get_all_milestone_progress()
Date parsing: YYYY-MM-DD format accepted and converted to datetime on store
Registered in src/kanboard_cli/main.py
CLI tests in tests/cli/test_milestone.py covering all 7 subcommands with mocked client and store
ruff check . && ruff format --check . passes
pytest passes
userStories.notes: add-task validation: call client.tasks.get_task(task_id), verify task.project_id is in portfolio.project_ids. Error clearly if task is in a project not belonging to the portfolio.

userStories.title: Dependency subcommands on portfolio CLI
userStories.description: Add 4 dependency subcommands to the existing portfolio CLI command group: dependencies, blocked, blocking, critical-path. These extend the portfolio command group (not a separate top-level group). All use DependencyAnalyzer against the full portfolio task set.
acceptanceCriteria: kanboard portfolio dependencies <name> [--cross-project-only] [--format graph|table|json]: defaults to ASCII graph via render_dependency_graph()
kanboard portfolio blocked <name>: table of task_id, title, project, blocked_by_task, blocked_by_project; all 4 output formats
kanboard portfolio blocking <name>: table of task_id, title, project, blocks_task, blocks_project; all 4 output formats
kanboard portfolio critical-path <name>: numbered list identifying bottleneck task (task whose completion unblocks the most downstream tasks)
--format graph|table|json on dependencies: graph=ASCII render, table=flat DependencyEdge rows, json=structured dict
blocked and blocking implicitly filter cross-project only (is_cross_project=True)
CLI tests in tests/cli/test_portfolio.py (extend existing file) covering all 4 subcommands
ruff check . && ruff format --check . passes
pytest passes
userStories.notes: dependencies fetches all portfolio tasks via PortfolioManager.get_portfolio_tasks() then passes to DependencyAnalyzer. bottleneck = task with highest count of transitively unblocked downstream tasks.

userStories.title: Unit tests — orchestration modules
userStories.description: Create comprehensive unit tests for all three orchestration modules: tests/unit/orchestration/test_store.py, test_portfolio.py, and test_dependencies.py. Shared fixtures in tests/unit/orchestration/conftest.py. Coverage targets: store.py >=95%, portfolio.py >=90%, dependencies.py >=90%.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/unit/orchestration/conftest.py with fixtures: sample_portfolio (3 projects, 2 milestones), mock_tasks (10 tasks across 3 projects), mock_task_links (cross-project blocking relationships), mock_link_types (standard Kanboard link types)

test_store.py: create/read/update/delete portfolios; milestones CRUD; task add/remove from milestone; atomic write; schema validation; missing file returns []; duplicate name raises ValueError; tmp_path isolation

test_portfolio.py: multi-project task aggregation; milestone progress at 0%/50%/100%; is_at_risk detection (within 7 days, <80%); is_overdue detection; missing project/task handling (warning, skip); sync_metadata writes correct keys

test_dependencies.py: linear chain critical path; diamond graph; no dependencies; cross-project filter; parallel independent chains; empty graph; single task no deps; cycle detection (warning, partial result); resolved vs unresolved edges; edge deduplication
pytest tests/unit/orchestration/ -v passes
Coverage: store.py >=95%, portfolio.py >=90%, dependencies.py >=90%
ruff check . && ruff format --check . passes
pytest passes
userStories.notes: Mock KanboardClient at the httpx level using pytest-httpx (consistent with existing test patterns). Use tmp_path for all store tests — never touch real ~/.config/kanboard/.

userStories.title: CLI tests — portfolio and milestone commands
userStories.description: Create CLI output tests for all portfolio and milestone commands using CliRunner. Tests cover all subcommands, all 4 output formats for list/table commands, error cases, and destructive command confirmation. Extend tests/cli/test_portfolio.py with dependency subcommand tests.
acceptanceCriteria: tests/cli/test_portfolio.py: all 8 CRUD subcommands + 4 dependency subcommands tested (at minimum success + error case each)
tests/cli/test_milestone.py: all 7 subcommands tested (at minimum success + error case each)
List commands tested with all 4 output formats (table/json/csv/quiet): portfolio list, portfolio tasks, milestone list
Error cases: portfolio not found, project does not exist (add-project), milestone not found, task not in portfolio project (add-task)
Destructive commands: --yes required verified by invoking without it and checking non-zero exit
portfolio show: milestone progress output present
portfolio dependencies: graph output contains expected task/project references
portfolio blocked / blocking: table output with correct columns
CLI tests use CliRunner(mix_stderr=False) to separate stdout and stderr for assertion
ruff check . && ruff format --check . passes
pytest passes
userStories.notes: Mock KanboardClient at the AppContext level and mock LocalPortfolioStore separately. Use same patching patterns as existing CLI tests in tests/cli/.

userStories.title: Task-link cross-project context enhancements
userStories.description: Enhance the existing task-link create command to display cross-project context when the two tasks belong to different projects. Add --with-project flag to task-link list to enrich each link with the opposite task's project name.
acceptanceCriteria: task-link create: after successful creation, if task.project_id != opposite_task.project_id, print informational message: 'ℹ Cross-project dependency: Task #<id> (<project>) is blocked by Task #<id> (<project>)'
Cross-project detection fetches both tasks only on success (no latency on error path)
task-link list --with-project: adds opposite_project column to output (requires one get_task() call per link)
--with-project documented as slower for tasks with many links
Existing task-link tests still pass (backward-compatible)
New tests for cross-project info message and --with-project flag
ruff check . && ruff format --check . passes
pytest passes
userStories.notes: Cross-project detection: call client.tasks.get_task() for both task_id and opposite_task_id after the link is created, compare project_id fields. Only on success to avoid adding latency to error paths.

userStories.title: Final wiring — exports, CLI registration, and integration verification
userStories.description: Wire the orchestration package into the public kanboard package exports and register portfolio and milestone command groups in main.py. Verify end-to-end: kanboard portfolio --help and kanboard milestone --help produce correct output. Run full test suite.
acceptanceCriteria: src/kanboard/__init__.py exports PortfolioManager, DependencyAnalyzer, LocalPortfolioStore (in sorted __all__)
from kanboard import PortfolioManager, DependencyAnalyzer, LocalPortfolioStore succeeds
src/kanboard_cli/main.py imports and registers portfolio and milestone command groups
kanboard portfolio --help lists: list, show, create, remove, add-project, remove-project, tasks, sync, dependencies, blocked, blocking, critical-path
kanboard milestone --help lists: list, show, create, remove, add-task, remove-task, progress
No duplicate command registration errors
ruff check . && ruff format --check . passes
pytest passes (full suite)
userStories.notes: Check __all__ sort order carefully — ruff RUF022 enforces alphabetical sort with capitals before lowercase. Run ruff check --fix to auto-correct.

userStories.title: Documentation — cross-project orchestration
userStories.description: Update README.md, docs/cli-reference.md, docs/sdk-guide.md, CLAUDE.md, and AGENTS.md to document the new orchestration features. Add portfolio and milestone sections to the CLI reference with usage examples. Add orchestration section to SDK guide. Update CLAUDE.md with orchestration architecture notes. BLOCKER: requires Kanboard Portfolio plugin URL from project owner before task 62 plugin-URL-dependent content can be written.

README.md: portfolio and milestone added to CLI command examples section

docs/cli-reference.md: portfolio command group documented with all 12 subcommands (list, show, create, remove, add-project, remove-project, tasks, sync, dependencies, blocked, blocking, critical-path)
docs/cli-reference.md: milestone command group documented with all 7 subcommands
docs/cli-reference.md: usage examples for common workflows (create portfolio, create milestone, view dependencies, check blockers)
docs/sdk-guide.md: new Cross-Project Orchestration section with PortfolioManager, DependencyAnalyzer, LocalPortfolioStore usage examples

CLAUDE.md: updated with src/kanboard/orchestration/ subpackage, ADR-16 note (orchestration is NOT resources), portfolio store path, kanboard_cli: metadata key prefix, reference to docs/design/cross-project-orchestration.md
